deep_dive.title: SAGE-OPD: Selective Agent-Guided Intervention for Multi-Turn On-Policy Distillation
deep_dive.abstract: On-policy distillation (OPD) improves student models by training them on trajectories induced by their own policy, making it a promising approach for mitigating exposure bias in agent training. However, most OPD studies focus on single-turn settings, while realistic LLM agents interact with environments over multiple turns. In this regime, early errors can alter future observations and compound across the trajectory, and standard dense token-level OPD becomes brittle, as it may over-penalize semantically valid alternatives, reinforce local degeneracies such as repeated actions, and propagate unreliable teacher supervision on off-distribution histories. We propose SAGE-OPD, a verifier-free selective intervention framework specifically designed for multi-turn OPD. Instead of applying teacher supervision uniformly across all turns, SAGE-OPD first observes environment feedback and uses teacher judgment to decide whether each student response should be skipped or intervened on. To further address compounding errors, SAGE-OPD weights token-level distillation by teacher confidence, reducing the influence of uncertain teacher distributions on corrupted or ambiguous histories. Finally, SAGE-OPD applies loss normalization to preserve the overall loss scale of standard OPD while retaining selective turn-level weighting. Experiments on agent tasks show that SAGE-OPD consistently improves over baselines, achieving up to a 13.3% relative improvement in ALFWorld unseen success rate over standard OPD. Ablation studies further demonstrate that turn-level intervention, teacher confidence weighting, and loss normalization provide complementary benefits. Our results suggest that effective multi-turn OPD should remain on-policy, but teacher supervision should be selectively allocated to turns where intervention is necessary and reliable.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Selective agent-guided intervention for multi-turn on-policy distillation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于多轮在线策略蒸馏的选择性智能体引导干预
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于多轮在线策略蒸馏的选择性智能体引导干预
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于多轮在线策略蒸馏的选择性智能体引导干预
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: SAGE-OPD proposes a verifier-free selective intervention framework to improve multi-turn on-policy distillation by providing teacher guidance only at critical steps, avoiding over-penalization and degeneracy.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文指出标准密集Token级在线策略蒸馏（OPD）在多轮环境下会过度惩罚语义合理的替代动作并强化局部退化，为此提出SAGE-OPD，一种无需验证器的选择性干预框架。SAGE-OPD仅在关键步骤提供教师指导，避免冗余监督，从而稳定多轮蒸馏。实验表明，该方法在多轮语言智能体任务上优于密集OPD基线，有效提升了学生模型在多轮交互中的鲁棒性和效率。该工作为后训练中高效应用在线策略蒸馏提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文指出标准密集Token级在线策略蒸馏（OPD）在多轮环境下会过度惩罚语义合理的替代动作并强化局部退化，为此提出SAGE-OPD，一种无需验证器的选择性干预框架。SAGE-OPD仅在关键步骤提供教师指导，避免冗余监督，从而稳定多轮蒸馏。实验表明，该方法在多轮语言智能体任务上优于密集OPD基线，有效提升了学生模型在多轮交互中的鲁棒性和效率。该工作为后训练中高效应用在线策略蒸馏提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: SPARK: Spatial Policy-driven Adaptive Reinforcement learning for Knowledge distillation
quick_skim.abstract: Low-bit quantization enables deployment of image restoration (IR) networks on resource-constrained devices, but introduces rounding noise that disproportionately degrades high-frequency regions such as edges and fine textures. Existing knowledge distillation (KD) methods apply distillation signals uniformly across all spatial locations, overlooking the varying reconstruction difficulty across image regions. To address this, we propose SPARK (Spatial Policy-driven Adaptive Reinforcement Learning for Knowledge Distillation), a framework that adaptively allocates distillation effort using a lightweight reinforcement learning (RL) policy network. At each training step, a difficulty feature extractor computes four signals, namely Laplacian variance, pixel variance, student reconstruction error, and teacher-student knowledge gap, which are fed into a compact policy CNN that produces a stochastic spatial weight map to modulate the KD loss during quantization-aware training (QAT). SPARK is IR task-agnostic, adds no inference cost, and integrates into any existing QAT pipeline without architectural changes. Experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate that SPARK consistently outperforms PTQ, QAT, and state-of-the-art (SOTA) KD approaches across multiple student architectures, achieving reconstruction quality closest to the full-precision teacher under significant computational constraints.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: RL policy network used for adaptive knowledge distillation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用强化学习策略网络自适应分配知识蒸馏力度
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用强化学习策略网络自适应分配知识蒸馏力度
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用强化学习策略网络自适应分配知识蒸馏力度
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SPARK uses a lightweight RL policy to adaptively allocate distillation effort across spatial regions based on reconstruction difficulty, improving low-bit quantized image restoration.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对低比特量化图像恢复中均匀蒸馏忽略区域难度差异的问题，本文提出SPARK框架，用轻量级强化学习策略网络根据像素难度自适应分配蒸馏信号。在多个图像恢复基准上，该方法优于均匀蒸馏方法，证明了策略驱动的知识蒸馏在计算机视觉任务中的有效性。尽管领域不同，其策略-蒸馏结合方式对策略蒸馏研究具有方法论参考价值。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对低比特量化图像恢复中均匀蒸馏忽略区域难度差异的问题，本文提出SPARK框架，用轻量级强化学习策略网络根据像素难度自适应分配蒸馏信号。在多个图像恢复基准上，该方法优于均匀蒸馏方法，证明了策略驱动的知识蒸馏在计算机视觉任务中的有效性。尽管领域不同，其策略-蒸馏结合方式对策略蒸馏研究具有方法论参考价值。

quick_skim.title: Skill-Guided Continuation Distillation for GUI Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Improving GUI agents typically relies on behavior cloning on expert trajectories. However, as the current policy deviates from the expert policy, it inevitably encounters policy-induced off-trajectory states during closed-loop execution, i.e., states that fall outside the expert trajectories. Since expert trajectories provide no demonstrations for these unseen states, such states receive no effective supervision, leaving the policy unable to select the correct action. To close this supervision gap, we propose Skill-Guided Continuation Distillation (SGCD), an iterative self-improvement framework. SGCD first runs the plain policy without skill guidance for a few steps to reach realistic off-trajectory states. From these states, a skill-guided policy then completes the task and produces successful continuations, which are mixed with expert trajectories to supply supervision over policy-induced off-trajectory states. The skills are extracted from both successful and failed rollouts, consisting of Continuation Plans, Critical Targets, Failure Traps, and Success Criteria. On OSWorld-Verified, SGCD improves the success rate of three base models from the low-30\% range to over 50\%, demonstrating its effectiveness and generality.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: policy distillation method for GUI agents using self-improvement
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于自改进的策略蒸馏方法用于GUI智能体
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于自改进的策略蒸馏方法用于GUI智能体
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于自改进的策略蒸馏方法用于GUI智能体
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SGCD addresses off-trajectory states in GUI agents by iteratively running the policy without skill guidance to reach realistic states, then using skill-guided completion for distillation, improving closed-loop performance.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对GUI智能体在闭环执行中遇到的策略诱导的偏离轨迹状态问题，本文提出技能引导连续蒸馏（SGCD）框架。该方法先运行无技能引导的简单策略以到达真实偏离状态，再由技能引导策略完成轨迹，从而弥补监督缺口。实验表明该方法显著提升了GUI智能体在未知状态下的动作选择能力，为策略蒸馏在交互式智能体中的应用提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对GUI智能体在闭环执行中遇到的策略诱导的偏离轨迹状态问题，本文提出技能引导连续蒸馏（SGCD）框架。该方法先运行无技能引导的简单策略以到达真实偏离状态，再由技能引导策略完成轨迹，从而弥补监督缺口。实验表明该方法显著提升了GUI智能体在未知状态下的动作选择能力，为策略蒸馏在交互式智能体中的应用提供了新思路。
